title: E.coli DH10B Competent Cell Preparation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"eb2r6","text":"Preparation of E. coli competent cells suitable for electroporation, to serve as hosts for subsequent plasmid introduction.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":15,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"2r0nj","text":"Cultured log-phase DH10B cells (200 mL culture, OD600 ≈ 0.6–0.8)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6fcj2","text":"Ice-cold sterile 10% glycerol solution (filter-sterilized, pre-chilled at 4 °C or on ice, prepare at least 100 mL)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"csofk","text":"Sterile centrifuge bottles (250–500 mL)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2lejo","text":"Sterile microcentrifuge tubes (1.5 mL, 10 tubes)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8eoll","text":"Sterile pipette tips and micropipettes","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8dbao","text":"Ice bucket and crushed ice bath (all procedures must be kept cold)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bkigu","text":"Refrigerated centrifuge (set to 4 °C, 4500 × g)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1pv5e","text":"Shaking incubator (37 °C, 220 rpm)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Obtain log-phase culture (OD600 ≈ 0.6–0.8) and immediately place it on ice to cool for 10 minutes (gently swirling during cooling is recommended).
Transfer 200 mL of culture into pre-chilled centrifuge bottles and centrifuge at 4500 × g for 10 minutes at 4 °C.
Carefully discard the supernatant without disturbing the cell pellet.
Add 50 mL of ice-cold 10% glycerol. Mix by repeatedly pipetting up and down until the suspension is evenly turbid. Centrifuge again at 4500 × g for 10 minutes at 4 °C.
Repeat steps 3–4 for a total of two washes.
After the final centrifugation, discard the supernatant and resuspend the entire pellet in 1 mL of ice-cold 10% glycerol. Transfer the suspension into sterile 1.5 mL tubes.
Aliquot 50–100 μL per tube. Keep on ice for immediate use, or snap-freeze in liquid nitrogen and store at −80 °C (stable for 1–2 weeks).
creator: igemnthuth
